fix: prevent double-escaped LaTeX in AI-generated quiz options

Two fixes for LaTeX rendering failures (e.g., "Misplaced &" in matrices):

1. fixCommonJsonIssues: preserve already-valid \\ pairs before fixing
   lone backslashes, preventing \\ → \\\ corruption that broke JSON
   parsing for LaTeX row separators

2. Add _normalizeLatexFields post-processing in validateAndFixQuestion
   to un-double \\begin → \begin, \\frac → \frac, etc. — catches both
   parser-induced and model-induced double escaping (Qwen/Ollama)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(proxy): fire discovery token retry for all GET 401s, not only when user auth present

Previously, the service token retry only fired when the browser had already sent
an Authorization header (has_user_auth_header=True). This meant unauthenticated
browsers (no token in localStorage) got a raw 401 propagated — Clusters, Data,
DeFi and Config tabs stayed empty.

Now the retry fires on every 401/403 for GET requests, using the Fedora discovery
token as fallback. Authenticated requests benefit first from their own JWT; if
Fedora rejects it the proxy falls back to discovery. Unauthenticated browsers also
get read-only data via discovery fallback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Add type×tier cross-cut table to diagnose which bet type + star combos lose money
- Add pre/post calibration split to measure calibration impact on ROI
- Raise totals minEV to 12 (separate from general minEV of 8)

fix(rag): use fully qualified lakehouse.schema.table names in SQL

When MotherDuck is attached (which happens when _get_md_conn runs),
DuckDB resolves unqualified schema.table lookups against the MotherDuck
default database. Using lakehouse.main_main.gold_match_summaries and
lakehouse.main.gold_match_embeddings routes queries unambiguously to
the local file regardless of what else is attached.

commit.message:
Fix gas payment address balance (#920)

* Fix gas payment to check address balance for all gas payers

Previously setGasPayment only checked address balance for sponsored
transactions (when gasData.owner was set). This caused "No valid gas
coins found" errors for accounts with sufficient address balance but
no coin objects. Now address balance is checked for all gas payers,
addresses are normalized for comparison, and gasPayer is explicitly
validated.

Also skips JSON RPC variant of the address balance test due to a
server-side bug where executeTransactionBlock returns empty
balanceChanges for payment: [] transactions, and updates sui-tools
image.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add json field to event data in transaction responses

When events: true is included, each event now contains a json field with the
JSON representation of the event's Move struct data (or null if unavailable).
Supported across all three transports (gRPC, GraphQL, JSON-RPC).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix zklogin e2e tests to use --json output for reliable parsing

The sui keytool zk-login-insecure-sign-personal-message command's table
output was not reliably parsed by the regex in CI with the newer
sui-tools image. Switch to --json flag for deterministic JSON output.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix zklogin e2e tests for new sui-tools image

The new sui-tools Docker image requires a wallet config at the default
path for `sui keytool` commands. This commit:
- Creates default sui config in globalSetup.ts before tests run
- Adds `execKeytool` helper with isolated keystores and retry logic
  to handle concurrent keytool calls that can crash the sui binary
- Uses `parseKeytoolJson` to robustly extract JSON from keytool output
  that may contain debug lines before the JSON object
- Removes invalid `--client.config` flag from keytool calls (keytool
  only accepts `--keystore-path`)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update sui-tools image and remove jsonrpc skip for address-balance test

The server-side bug causing empty balanceChanges for address-balance-backed
gas transactions was fixed in MystenLabs/sui#25685. Update the Docker image
to include the fix and remove the test skip.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Send full cave to Celestin with query-ranked ordering

Instead of sending only top 24 generically-ranked bottles, send the
entire cave ranked by relevance to the user's message. This ensures
Celestin can answer questions about any bottle (e.g. "do I have Savart?")
while keeping the most relevant bottles at the top of the prompt.

commit.message:
audit kmp roadmap AjuNk (#106)

* feat(audit): implement Tier-2 operational logging + audit log enhancements (TODO-004 Phase 1)

## Tier-2 Operational Logging Stack
- Add `OperationalLogRepository` interface (domain layer) with insert, paginated
  read, and retention-policy purge methods
- Add `OperationalLogRepositoryImpl` (data layer) mapping `operational_logs.sq`
  SQLDelight queries to domain model
- Add `KermitSqliteAdapter` (data layer) bridging Kermit `LogWriter` → SQLite via
  fire-and-forget coroutines; exceptions swallowed so logging never crashes the app
- Add `LogRetentionJob` (data layer) coroutine-based daily purge enforcing policy:
  VERBOSE/DEBUG 3d · INFO 14d · WARN 30d · ERROR/FATAL 90d
- Register `OperationalLogRepository`, `KermitSqliteAdapter`, `LogRetentionJob`
  in `DataModule` Koin bindings

## Brute-Force Detection
- Add `SecurityAuditLogger.isLoginBruteForced()`: queries `getRecentLoginFailureCount`
  and returns true if ≥5 failures within a 5-minute window
- Wire brute-force check in `AuthViewModel.onLoginClicked()`: shows dedicated
  "Too many failed attempts" message after threshold is exceeded

## Audit Log Viewer Enhancements (AuditLogScreen / AdminViewModel)
- Add `auditEventTypeFilter` and `auditSuccessFilter` fields to `AdminState`
- Add `FilterAuditByEventType`, `FilterAuditBySuccess`, `ExportAuditLogCsv` intents
- Add `ShareCsvExport` effect (carries RFC-4180 CSV content + suggested filename)
- Implement `exportAuditLogCsv()` in `AdminViewModel`: generates CSV of filtered
  entries with RFC-4180 quoting and emits `ShareCsvExport` effect
- Update client-side filter in `AuditLogScreen` to apply all three filter dimensions
- Add Material 3 `ExposedDropdownMenuBox` for event type (39 entries + "All")
- Add `FilterChip` row for success/fail 3-way toggle (All / OK / FAIL)
- Add CSV export `IconButton` (Download icon) in the filter toolbar

https://claude.ai/code/session_0195xibztDGTtaHvqNKVstKz

* build(gradle): fix proxy nonProxyHosts + Clock import for LogRetentionJob

## Proxy fix (gradle.properties)
Sandboxed environments set JAVA_TOOL_OPTIONS with *.google.com and
*.googleapis.com in nonProxyHosts, causing the JVM to bypass the proxy
for maven.google.com. Direct connections fail inside the sandbox, so
AGP and other Google Maven artifacts could not be resolved.

Added explicit nonProxyHosts override in org.gradle.jvmargs that removes
the *.google.com exclusion, routing maven.google.com through the Anthropic
egress proxy like all other Maven repos. Production CI (GitHub Actions)
does not use a proxy, so this change is a no-op in CI.

## Clock import fix (LogRetentionJob.kt)
Changed `import kotlinx.datetime.Clock` to `import kotlin.time.Clock` to
match the convention used by all other files in :shared:data. kotlinx-datetime
is not a direct dependency of :shared:data and Clock.System was not resolving
on the androidMain compilation target.

https://claude.ai/code/session_0195xibztDGTtaHvqNKVstKz

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: system audit — transaction safety, memory, null guards

- Re-extract: wrap delete+reset in $transaction with cascade-safe
  deletion (RecipeEditHistory + RecipeSnapshot before Recipe).
  Prevents orphaned records and foreign key violations.

- Upload chunk assembly: stream to temp file via Bun.file().writer()
  instead of Buffer[] + Buffer.concat. Halves peak memory usage

commit.message:
fix: NumberFire + VSiN scrapers via Playwright JS rendering

NumberFire: domain redirects to FanDuel React SPA. Override
download_and_decode() with Playwright to render client-side JS,
wait for projection table, extract rendered HTML.

VSiN: WordPress AJAX-loaded betting splits. Same Playwright pattern
with networkidle wait + extra delay for AJAX data population.

Both use playwright-stealth for bot detection avoidance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: thread nodeForwarder through sendBatch to avoid racy map lookup (gastrolog-3faz)

logFailure was acquiring rf.mu to look up the nodeForwarder from the
map, releasing it, then reading nf.failures — which looked like a
cross-goroutine race even though the entire call chain runs within a
single flushLoop goroutine.

Thread nf directly through sendBatch → logFailure so the backoff
fields are accessed without any map lookup or lock, making the
single-goroutine access pattern self-evident.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: eliminate double fetchProfile call and add retry on AbortError

Three issues were causing login failures:
1. initAuth() + onAuthStateChange(INITIAL_SESSION) both called fetchProfile
   simultaneously when a session existed, sending two identical requests
   to the backend. Supabase v2 fires INITIAL_SESSION synchronously on
   listener registration, making initAuth() redundant.
2. Backend cold-start on Render free tier dropped both connections
   (NS_BINDING_ABORTED), leaving profile=null and navigation never firing.
3. No retry logic meant a single failed fetchProfile left the user stuck.

Fix:
- Remove initAuth() entirely; rely on INITIAL_SESSION event
- Filter TOKEN_REFRESHED to prevent spurious re-fetches
- Add setLoading(true) before fetchProfile so PublicRoute shows spinner
- Add retry logic (3 attempts, 1.5/3/4.5s delays) on AbortError
- useCallback for stable fetchProfile reference
